expectations.text: Engages with the plan's specific PocketBase rationale (pb_hooks for server logic, single binary / two deployable pieces, migrations as code) rather than a generic Supabase-vs-PocketBase feature comparison
expectations.evidence: Argues from pb_hooks/goja limitations (no npm, no Stripe SDK, hand-rolled webhook verification), the section 7.2 CI bootstrap, the plan's own SSR-auth hedge, and the two-deployable-pieces property.

expectations.text: Acknowledges the user's timing point — that pre-Phase-0 is the cheapest moment to switch — rather than dismissing the question
expectations.evidence: "You have zero commits... the switching cost right now is rewriting sections 2-5 of the plan (call it one evening to a day) and zero lines of code. At Phase 4 it would be weeks."

expectations.text: Presents at least two genuine alternatives / framings (e.g. switch now, stay, or a criteria-based tripwire for switching later)
expectations.evidence: Switch now (recommended) vs the honest case for staying (VPS cost, cron, SQLite adequacy, sunk planning) — plus a motive gut-check condition before switching.

expectations.text: Commits to a single clear recommendation rather than a neutral pros/cons list
expectations.evidence: "My recommendation: switch, now" with two explicit conditions and 'this is the last free re-litigation.'

expectations.text: Weighs the decision against solo part-time development reality (ops burden, hosting cost, learning curve) with concrete specifics
expectations.evidence: €5/mo Hetzner vs $25/mo Supabase Pro (free tier pausing flagged as a dealbreaker for live quote links), 'fewer things broken at 11pm on a Tuesday', ~90% of the plan surviving, one-day translation cost.

notes: Went beyond the user's framing to the strongest Supabase argument (payments code in goja).
Anti-churn guard: distinguishes ops-risk motive from shiny-tech motive.
Offered docs/decisions.md record and asked before touching the plan document, per the skill.
